questions.title: Supported Order Types
questions.context: Phase 2 requires order placement (TRADE-01). Must balance realism vs complexity. Backend currently has price feed infrastructure via ProviderRegistry.

options.label: Market orders only
options.description: Simplest to implement. Buy/sell at current price. Good for MVP but limits trading strategies.

options.label: Market + Limit orders
options.description: Covers 90% of retail trading. Moderate complexity. Recommended balance.

options.label: Market + Limit + Stop Loss + Take Profit
options.description: Full TRADE-01 coverage. Higher complexity - requires trigger monitoring and conditional execution.

options.label: Custom
options.description: Specify your own set

questions.title: Order Fill Model
questions.context: How orders execute affects realism vs performance. Current price feeds update every few seconds via WebSocket.

options.label: Immediate fill at current price
options.description: Order fills instantly using latest price. Simple, no simulation queue. Best for paper trading MVP.

options.label: Simulated delay with price slippage
options.description: Add realistic 50-500ms delay + random slippage (0.01-0.1%). Addresses TRADE-04 latency requirement.

options.label: Full exchange simulation with order book
options.description: Simulated bid/ask spread, depth-based fills. Highest realism, significant complexity. Overkill for v1.

questions.title: Partial Fills
questions.context: TRADE-03 requires handling partial fills. Affects how open orders display and how P&L calculates.

options.label: No partial fills - all or nothing
options.description: Simpler order state model. One fill record per order. Not realistic but easier to implement.

options.label: Support partial fills
options.description: Order can be 30% filled, 70% open. Requires fill records per execution. More realistic, moderate complexity.

questions.title: Position Tracking Method
questions.context: PORT-01 requires portfolio tracking. How positions aggregate matters for P&L and portfolio view.

options.label: Simple - current quantity per symbol
options.description: Track total shares/coins held. Display current value. Lose cost basis history for P&L.

options.label: FIFO lots - track each purchase separately
options.description: Each buy creates a lot. Sell uses oldest first. Accurate realized P&L, standard tax method.

options.label: Average cost basis
options.description: Track total quantity and average purchase price. Simplest P&L, common for portfolio tracking.

questions.title: Multi-Currency Handling
questions.context: Assets trade in USD, EUR, etc but user views in INR per CLAUDE.md constraints.

options.label: Single base currency (INR only)
options.description: Convert everything to INR at transaction time. Simple portfolio view, all values in INR.

options.label: Track original currency + live conversion
options.description: Store original USD/EUR amounts. Convert on-the-fly for display. More accurate but complex.

questions.title: Cash Reservation for Open Orders
questions.context: When order placed, is cash reserved? Or only deducted on fill? Affects available balance display.

options.label: Reserve on order placement
options.description: Cash deducted when order placed, released if cancelled. Prevents overspending. More realistic.

options.label: Deduct on fill only
options.description: Cash stays available until order executes. Risk of overspending if multiple orders placed. Simpler.

questions.title: Starting Capital
questions.context: CLAUDE.md specifies 100K INR starting capital. Any flexibility?

options.label: Fixed 100K INR
options.description: Hardcoded per spec. Simple, no config needed.

options.label: Configurable initial capital
options.description: Env var or setting. Allows testing with different amounts. Slight complexity.

options.label: Multiple portfolio support
options.description: User can create multiple portfolios with different capitals. More complex, enables A/B testing.

questions.title: Trade Detail Level
questions.context: PORT-02 requires trade history table. What details per trade?

options.label: Basic: symbol, qty, price, timestamp
options.description: Clean simple view. Covers essentials only.

options.label: Detailed: add P&L per trade, order type, execution time
options.description: Rich view with realized P&L, order type, latency. Better for analysis.

options.label: Full audit: order ID, fills, status transitions
options.description: Complete lifecycle view. Overkill for most users but good for debugging.

questions.title: P&L Display
questions.context: PORT-03 requires performance metrics. What to show?

options.label: Total P&L only
options.description: Simple net gain/loss. Easy to understand, minimal calculation.

options.label: Realized + Unrealized P&L
options.description: Separate closed trades vs open positions. Standard trading view, moderate complexity.

options.label: Full metrics: P&L, win rate, avg gain/loss, max drawdown
options.description: Complete trading statistics. Best for serious trading review, most complex.

questions.title: Daily/Session Reset
questions.context: PORT-04 requires portfolio reset. Also useful for daily P&L tracking.

options.label: Full reset to initial capital
options.description: Clears all positions, resets cash. Simple implementation.

options.label: Archive current portfolio, start fresh
options.description: Keep history but start new tracking period. Enables performance over time analysis.

options.label: Daily P&L snapshot only
options.description: No reset, just mark day-end values. Track daily performance without losing positions.

questions.title: Order Validity (Time in Force)
questions.context: TRADE-02 requires viewing/canceling open orders. How long do limit orders stay open?

options.label: GTC - Good Till Canceled
options.description: Orders stay open until filled or manually cancelled. Most common for limit orders.

options.label: Day orders only
options.description: Orders auto-cancel at session end. Simpler state management, requires session concept.

options.label: Both GTC and Day
options.description: User chooses. Flexible, moderate complexity.

questions.title: Order Modification
questions.context: Can users modify open orders? Change limit price or quantity?

options.label: Cancel/replace only
options.description: Must cancel and create new order. Simpler, standard pattern.

options.label: In-place modification
options.description: Edit price/qty directly. Better UX, more complex state tracking.
